cross_references.note: AskUserQuestion was used before proceeding with implementation — correct disambiguation behavior; no terse-approval misclassification observed this unit
Benchmark results written to findings.md appear to reflect actual tool output rather than synthesized values; no hallucination detected; insight not violated

recommendations: shell_tool_preference_violations: No new recommendation — flagged in six consecutive cycles with zero trajectory improvement; structural fixture. Mechanical enforcement (PreToolUse hook blocking Bash for cat/head/ls on known file extensions) is the only lever that will move this metric; agent-layer reminders are not working.

mcp_retry_without_diagnosis: No new recommendation — five consecutive cycles at count=2; 'possibly_improving' trajectory claim from cycle-3 is now definitively unconfirmed and should be retired; pattern is stable-bad, not improving.
compound_command_overreach: No new recommendation — STABLE plateau for three cycles; single decompose-after-failure per cycle is not worsening but also not improving.

bash_retry_without_diagnosis: Pattern held stable at single occurrence per cycle; ATONE_INDEX_OK=1 env prefix retry specifically suggests the agent is pattern-matching a workaround rather than diagnosing the stash hook cause — worth investigating whether git stash is legitimately blocked by a hook and documenting the correct invocation path.

optimistic_write_without_verification: Second consecutive cycle at same failure rate; agent should Read/Bash-check target path existence before Write to unknown paths in tests/ directories where prior session state is uncertain.

unit-33 is a positive signal: when task shape is research (web search + synthesis), tool selection is clean and calibration is high (0.75). The pathological patterns cluster exclusively on shell/filesystem operations.

unit_assessments.notes: Pure text response, no tools. Calibration neutral — no outcome signal to assess against.
